title: Transforming social protection for irreversible climate change-induced loss and damage: key roles for philanthropy
content: This briefing paper examines the role of social protection systems in responding to irreversible climate-induced loss and damage.
snippet: This briefing paper examines the role of social protection systems in responding to irreversible climate-induced loss and damage.

title: How extreme temperatures and humidity raise health risks
content: Extreme temperatures, both hot and cold, can lead to increased mortality, and there are many examples of such events.
snippet: Extreme temperatures, both hot and cold, can lead to increased mortality, and there are many examples of such events.

title: El Niño likely to return: the case for early action
content: Climate models are converging: El Niño is likely to return by mid-2026 and could be strong.
snippet: Climate models are converging: El Niño is likely to return by mid-2026 and could be strong.

title: Ministry of Education and MDMC strengthen school preparedness through training of disaster safe and psychosocial education units in schools

title: Scaling urban nature-based solutions across Czech cities and landscapes: research and practitioner perspectives
content: This webinar is organized in the framework of the NBSINFRA project funded by the European Union.
snippet: This webinar is organized in the framework of the NBSINFRA project funded by the European Union.

title: Thirty years of earthquake retrofit in the city of Los Angeles: Trends, drivers, and implications for seismic resilience
content: This study examines three decades of seismic retrofit activity in the City of Los Angeles to assess how the implementation has varied across space, time, and socially vulnerable communities.
snippet: This study examines three decades of seismic retrofit activity in the City of Los Angeles to assess how the implementation has varied across space, time, and socially vulnerable communities.

title: Cyclone Gabrielle exposed the risks of forestry slash. New research suggests little has changed
content: When Cyclone Gabrielle tore through New Zealand’s in 2023, it left behind more than silt and floodwaters. Rivers were choked with forestry debris, beaches littered with logs, and homes, bridges and farmland buried under tonnes of forestry slash

title: Hantavirus: the ‘silent’ virus
content: As a hantavirus outbreak makes global headlines, CSIRO researchers are investigating how these rodent-borne viruses spread and why different strains target specific human organs, like the lungs or kidneys, with such varying levels of severity.

title: What is non-economic loss and damage and why does it matter?
content: Explaining non‑economic loss and damage: how climate change affects lives, identity, culture and belonging in ways numbers can’t capture.
snippet: Explaining non‑economic loss and damage: how climate change affects lives, identity, culture and belonging in ways numbers can’t capture.

title: This summer, the American water crisis becomes real
content: Concerns over water access are poised to consume summer in the U.S., as crises in Corpus Christi and across the Colorado River threaten to boil over.
snippet: Concerns over water access are poised to consume summer in the U.S., as crises in Corpus Christi and across the Colorado River threaten to boil over.

title: Advancing gender equity and climate resilience through baobab fruit value chains
content: This case study explores how the commercialisation of baobab fruit in northern Mozambique is creating new opportunities for rural women while strengthening climate resilience.
snippet: This case study explores how the commercialisation of baobab fruit in northern Mozambique is creating new opportunities for rural women while strengthening climate resilience.

title: Integration of city-centric approaches into national policy processes: Kampala’s case
content: This case study examines how Kampala, Uganda's capital, is integrating city-centric approaches into national policy to manage climate-related human mobility
snippet: This case study examines how Kampala, Uganda's capital, is integrating city-centric approaches into national policy to manage climate-related human mobility

title: Insurance and the Sustainable Development Goals
content: Learn how insurance can drive progress toward the SDGs, highlighting the tangible contributions of insurance companies and their role in advancing the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development through transformational innovations and partnerships.

title: Reinterpreting disasters and urban resilience in the Anthropocene: Disaster management or transforming with disasters?
content: This study aims to reinterpret disasters from the perspective of Anthropocene and discuss its implications for disaster policies.
snippet: This study aims to reinterpret disasters from the perspective of Anthropocene and discuss its implications for disaster policies.

title: Sint Maarten national climate change adaptation report
content: This National Climate Change Adaptation (NCCA) Report serves as the bridge between recognizing those vulnerabilities and enforcing resilience.
snippet: This National Climate Change Adaptation (NCCA) Report serves as the bridge between recognizing those vulnerabilities and enforcing resilience.

title: Seismic attenuation techniques reveal what lies beneath Taiwan
content: A new imaging modality explores the complex subduction‐collision transition zone below southern Taiwan.
snippet: A new imaging modality explores the complex subduction‐collision transition zone below southern Taiwan.

title: Why ocean warming experiments may be making misleading predictions
content: Accurate experiments on how ocean warming affects marine life are vital to ensure we can best prepare for the future, protect our food sources, and help safeguard ocean ecosystems.
snippet: Accurate experiments on how ocean warming affects marine life are vital to ensure we can best prepare for the future, protect our food sources, and help safeguard ocean ecosystems.

title: Inland seas around the world are severely affected by climate change

title: Drivers of Antarctica’s dramatic sea ice decline could accelerate global warming: new research
content: For decades, Antarctic sea ice appeared resistant to global warming. Now, scientists say a cascade of ocean and atmospheric changes has ‘flipped the system’ – and the consequences could accelerate climate change worldwide.

title: At the edge of (un)inhabitability? Spatial diversity of demographic vulnerability to increasing heat exposure across remote towns of Northern Australia
content: The study provides a spatially detailed picture of the diversity in demographic vulnerability to increasing heat which is projected from climate change models for remote towns in Northern Australia, in both the immediate and long-term.

title: A hybrid approach to earthquake-preconditioned debris flow: regional susceptibility and event-based hazard assessments
content: This study presents the an integrated debris flow susceptibility & numerical modelling framework applied under post-seismic conditions in Türkiye. The Tut region of Adıyaman in southeastern Türkiye experienced destructive debris flow events in 2023
snippet: This study presents the an integrated debris flow susceptibility & numerical modelling framework applied under post-seismic conditions in Türkiye. The Tut region of Adıyaman in southeastern Türkiy

title: Pastoral adaptation to prolonged drought in Morocco: Household strategies among Amazigh nomadic communities
content: Case study examining how Amazigh pastoral households in Morocco adapted nomadic livelihoods during a seven-year drought through informal water access and livestock restructuring.
snippet: Case study examining how Amazigh pastoral households in Morocco adapted nomadic livelihoods during a seven-year drought through informal water access and livestock restructuring.

title: A catalogue of nature‑based solutions for coastal resilience
content: The Coastal Catalogue is designed to support the identification of investment opportunities and the integration of NBS into coastal risk management and planning processes.
snippet: The Coastal Catalogue is designed to support the identification of investment opportunities and the integration of NBS into coastal risk management and planning processes.

title: Assessing El Niño definitions and early warning alerts across Australia, Peru, and the U.S.: implications for disaster risk reduction and climate justice
content: This study examined how three Pacific Rim countries issued early warning alerts associated with El Niño, the warm phase of the climate cycle, El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO).
snippet: This study examined how three Pacific Rim countries issued early warning alerts associated with El Niño, the warm phase of the climate cycle, El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO).

title: Democracy meets extreme heat: the end of summer elections?
content: Across India and beyond, voters are being asked to go to the polls in dangerously high temperatures, with democracy as well as their health at risk
snippet: Across India and beyond, voters are being asked to go to the polls in dangerously high temperatures, with democracy as well as their health at risk

title: Inequality causing 100,000 extra deaths a year from heat and cold in Europe
content: Findings come after third-hottest April on record globally and amid fears of more brutal European summer weather
snippet: Findings come after third-hottest April on record globally and amid fears of more brutal European summer weather

title: A vital Atlantic current is fading far faster, threatening Europe, Africa and North America by 2100
content: A key Atlantic Ocean current system that helps regulate the planet's climate could weaken more than expected by 2100, with potentially devastating consequences worldwide, a new study has found.
snippet: A key Atlantic Ocean current system that helps regulate the planet's climate could weaken more than expected by 2100, with potentially devastating consequences worldwide, a new study has found.

title: Why some Europeans face deadlier heat and cold: Inequality map reveals who is most at risk
content: Regions with greater socioeconomic inequalities are more affected by cold weather, whereas areas with higher levels of wealth and urbanization are at greater risk during heat waves and lower risk during cold spells.

title: Anticipating wildfires: new AI-enhanced risk intelligence
content: Understanding and anticipating wildfires is no longer optional; it is foundational to effective preparedness, response, and resilience.
snippet: Understanding and anticipating wildfires is no longer optional; it is foundational to effective preparedness, response, and resilience.

title: Building resilience that lasts: game changers for a livable planet
content: Storms, floods, heat, and droughts hit hardest those who have the least.  When the soil cracks and the rivers dry, work disappears. In Sub-Saharan Africa, one drought can wipe out 900,000 jobs in a year — and with them, years of progress.

title: Consultant for Project Support: INSPIRE India
content: The program aims to enhance institutional systems for disaster preparedness and climate action at the national and state levels, while contributing to regional cooperation in the Bay of Bengal.
snippet: The program aims to enhance institutional systems for disaster preparedness and climate action at the national and state levels, while contributing to regional cooperation in the Bay of Bengal.

title: Sensing the sounds from earth’s hazardous environments
content: Low-cost infrasound sensors, deployed in large numbers, provide a practical means of data collection near volcanoes, earthquakes, wildfires, and other geophysical phenomena.
snippet: Low-cost infrasound sensors, deployed in large numbers, provide a practical means of data collection near volcanoes, earthquakes, wildfires, and other geophysical phenomena.

content: As wildfires increase in frequency and intensity across regions like the western United States, smoke exposure is becoming more widespread and prolonged. Yet current knowledge gaps make it harder to develop targeted interventions or guidelines

title: Volcano cleans up after itself by removing methane from the air
content: A giant volcanic eruption may have accidentally uncovered a powerful new way to destroy methane and slow global warming
snippet: A giant volcanic eruption may have accidentally uncovered a powerful new way to destroy methane and slow global warming

title: How a repurposed medical device is helping us investigate ancient climate tipping points
content: A tool designed to scan blood now offers us an exciting opportunity to read Earth’s history in finer detail and decipher hidden mechanisms.
snippet: A tool designed to scan blood now offers us an exciting opportunity to read Earth’s history in finer detail and decipher hidden mechanisms.

title: Ecuador | Volcanic Ash: Early Action Protocol Annual Report (EAP2023EC03| MDREC024)
content: This report summarizes the annual readiness and prepositioning activities implemented during year two (2025), covering the period from 02 January 2025 to 31 December 2025.
snippet: This report summarizes the annual readiness and prepositioning activities implemented during year two (2025), covering the period from 02 January 2025 to 31 December 2025.

title: Phase of El Niño-Southern Oscillation foreshadows U.S. wildfire activity up to a year in advance
